title: iChemistry SDS API: drop Header fields on POST (do not persist)
description:
Problem

On POST to the iChemistry SDS API, the three Header fields — Version, Revised, Replaces SDS — are stored, but the API mangles them on the way in. Because the persisted values can't be trusted, the EntryTool team wants to fill them in manually from the source SDS instead.

Fields in scope

From the SDS payload schema we post against:

"Header": {

```
"type": "object",
"additionalProperties": false,
"properties": {
  "Version":      { "type": "string" },
  "Revised":      { "type": "string" },
  "Replaces SDS": { "type": "string" }
},
"required": ["Version", "Revised", "Replaces SDS"]
```

}

Ask

On POST, discard Header.Version, Header.Revised, and Header.Replaces SDS. Don't persist them, don't surface them anywhere downstream — treat them as if they weren't sent. Leave the corresponding stored fields empty so EntryTool can populate them manually.

The fields can still be accepted in the request body for backwards compatibility (no schema break), but they should be silently dropped server-side.

Why

EntryTool will own these three fields manually going forward. The current behaviour — accepting the values and then overwriting them incorrectly — is worse than not accepting them at all, because EntryTool then has to undo whatever the API decided.

Acceptance

* The API does not store these three fields from the POST endpoint.

title: Chemsoft SDS - Updates for existing products
description:
iChemistry API currently exports SDS data (SDSCom) from MultiDB for product data for products from MultiDB_Explizit that has been added/inventoried or modified after specific date in the request.

Normally Chemsoft requests products changed since 3 days before current date/time.

SDSHubProductImport tool saves the result as XML file and creates new products that currently not found in Chemsoft (Chemsoft_ExplizitDB) and it compares the product by date.

If product with same SDS date already exists, it skips the product and proceeds to next product in the XML.

This is a problem when SDS admins modifies existing product (corrections or adds missing information) and these changes are not updated in Chemsoft databases.

We need to find a way around this to enable continuous updates for existing products without creating new products (versions) in Chemsoft database (only update the data for existing version) and we also need to modify stored procedure that distributes SDS data in client databases so it doesn't create duplicate product.

title: Updated version of the scheduled task for IIS to HubSpot sync
description:
Hi John,

Background (for the ticket):
 I would like some help deploying an exe that should run every 30 minutes on IntSqlServer01. It reads from our SQL databases and pushes data to HubSpot over HTTPS.

However, I don't want to bake in the credentials in the executable. From my understanding here is what we want:

* 

A service account for the sync to run as
   \*
 A SQL login for that Windows account on IntSqlServer01 with the same permissions the existing lime_sync login has today. It needs read across lime_crm, Intersolia_Internal_System_CRM, Intersolia_Common_Userbase, Intersolia_Internal_System_History, sales_reports, and write on Accounts_Main.hs_companyid, Orders_HubspotTicketIDs, and lime_crm..HubSpotSyncData.
   \*
 A Windows Scheduled Task on IntSqlServer01: every 30 minutes, runs HubspotSync.exe.

title: FD 318759 - Error ID 18453051 för riskbedömning
Uppgradera EuPhrac v5.5 till v5.7
description:
Vi har fått en uppdatering på EuPhrac frasbibliotek som behöver in i databasen.

Det finns (mig veterligen) ingen dokumentation om hur man gjort detta tidigare, så det kommer krävas undersökning om hur vi går tillväga.

Frasbiblioteket som ska uppgraderas är "EuPhrac 5.5" (LibraryGuid: "{3C21B474-3ED0-4803-9318-EEF94FC1E5C8}") och nya fraser finns i bifogad zip.

Varje EuPhrac fras har ett PhraseID som är kopplat till värdet i localRegML.ImportedPhraseID. Finns redan frasen i databasen ska Phrase-kolumnen uppdateras med den nya frasen från csv-filen (befintligt localRegML.PhraseID måste behållas). Finns inte frasen i databasen ska den läggas till (nytt unikt värde för localRegML.PhraseID måste skapas).

Sedan döper vi om "EuPhrac 5.5" till "EuPhrac 5.7" i PhraseLibrary-tabellen.

[20260427_eSDScom_5_7_Core.zip](https://uploads.linear.app/d040f0e9-edba-4253-a021-5028751c4dfb/f3d3bf1a-1b36-4d5d-af2d-502b696678d8/0ff78a73-9acf-4d43-a30c-1c5b78dac35a)

title: SQL injection in ClearFilesFromWebSession
description:
## Summary

Filenames from user uploads are interpolated unsanitized into a raw SQL DELETE against WebAppSession. Filenames containing an apostrophe (e.g. ... SEAL'N'FLEX ... .pdf) break the query and constitute a SQL injection vector.

## Location

Common/iChemistry.DataAccess/Queries/ClearFilesFromWebSession.cs:25

```
session.CreateSQLQuery(
  $"delete from WebAppSession where SessionHash = '{SessionHash}' " +
  $"and StringData in ({string.Join(",", FileNames.Select(f => $"'{f}'"))})")
```

The same pattern exists in ClearWebSession.cs:17 (parameter is an int there, so lower risk — but should be fixed for

consistency).

## Reproduction

1. Open a risk assessment
2. Upload a document whose filename contains an apostrophe, e.g. Kemikaliegranskningsprotokoll BOSTIK H760 SEAL'N'FLEX PRO SEAL BLACK 260310.pdf.
3. Save the form.
4. Observe SQL error: Incorrect syntax near 'N'FLEX PRO SEAL BLACK 260310.pdf'.

## Impact

* Functional: Saving a risk assessment fails for filenames containing apostrophes; staging rows in WebAppSession are not cleaned up.
* Security: SQL injection. The filename is user-controlled; an authenticated user can smuggle arbitrary SQL executed against WebAppSession (and potentially beyond, depending on DB permissions).

## Call sites

SaveDocuments in the following controllers, invoked after upload:

* RiskAssessmentMainController.cs:941
* RiskAssessmentHseController.cs:715
* RiskAssessmentWorkPermitController.cs:219
* RiskAssessmentMeasurementController.cs:163
* RiskAssessmentEmergencyPlanController.cs:164

## Proposed fix

Switch to a parameterized query.

Handle empty FileNames (NHibernate does not allow an empty SetParameterList) with an early return. Apply the same treatment in ClearWebSession.cs.

## Acceptance criteria

* Filenames containing ', ;, --, etc. are saved/cleared correctly with no SQL errors.
* Regression test uploading a file with an apostrophe in its name through the full RiskAssessment flow.
* Code review confirms no other CreateSQLQuery($"...") interpolations have been introduced.

## Severity

High - authenticated SQL injection plus functional blocker.

title: Investigate few products missing technical symbols for Ringhals sandbox2
description:
Mail from Caroline: 

**Produkter som tidigare haft teknisk märkning i Ringhals system saknar nu denna märkning**.

**Exempel:**

Produkt **i16986** har teknisk märkning i **main/ringhals**, men saknar märkning i **sandbox2/ringhals**. Produkten redigerades senast **2025‑12‑10**.
Samma produkt finns även inventerad i **main/forsmark** och **sandbox2/forsmark**, där den fortfarande har korrekt märkning (**4**).

**Teori:**
Det har tidigare sett ut som att Ringhals produkter haft egen märkning, men i praktiken kan detta ha varit Forsmarks märkning som visats. Efter att databaserna separerats (splittats) blir det tydligt att vissa produkter i Ringhals system faktiskt saknar egen märkning.

Conclusion after checking databases:

Vad gäller **i16986**, misstänker jag att det är så enkelt att det är en produkt någon har testat att ändra symboler på. Ringhals-märkningen ligger nämligen kvar i original-databasen som sandboxarna är baserad på.

Jag jämförde datan för ringhals teknisk märkning mot backupen, och endast följande produkter saknar eller har annan märkning:

i6556

i13780

i16986

i55682

Request for Installation of Azure DevOps Agents on All Web Servers
description:
Dear Team,
 I hope this message finds you well.
 In the interest of further strengthening the stability, efficiency, and overall professionalism of our deployment process, I would like to most respectfully request the installation of Azure DevOps self-hosted agents on each of our web servers.
 Our current deployment procedure involves a considerable amount of manual coordination across multiple load-balanced servers, including health check management, server rotation handling, application warm-up, and version synchronization. While this process has served us reliably, it remains time-consuming and introduces unnecessary operational overhead during each release.
 By establishing Azure DevOps agents directly on all web servers, we would be able to significantly streamline and accelerate deployments through controlled, automated rollouts. This would allow us to:

* Deploy sequentially across servers with precision and minimal manual intervention
* Reduce release time substantially
* Lower the risk of human error during production deployments
* Ensure consistent artifact delivery across all nodes
* Support safer warm-up and validation steps before reintroducing servers into rotation

Given that these servers are already hosted within Azure, this enhancement would provide an elegant and highly maintainable path toward a modern deployment workflow aligned with best operational practices.
 I would be sincerely grateful if this installation could be prioritized, as it would materially improve both deployment speed and release confidence going forward.
 Please let me know if any additional technical details, access requirements, or implementation guidance are needed from my side.
 With kind regards,
 Christoffer

title: FD 314850 - Anedningar syns ej för i19430 i Substitutionsmodulen (OCH visas dubbelt i första vyn)
Optimize batch custom field updates to reduce database writes
description:
UpdateProductFields in CustomFieldsController currently saves each product to the database once per field being updated. Updating 5 fields on 2 products results in 10 saves instead of 2.

Apply all field value changes in memory first, then save each product once. This reduces the number of DB round-trips from fields × products to just products, lowering lock duration and contention on the local product table.

Fix "Product not found in local table" error in CustomFields API
description:
Products copied via flex application flow likely missing localMainRegArticle row, causing API errors

## Problem:

Customers report intermittent "Product not found in local table" errors when calling UpdateProductFields via the Custom Fields API. Example: product ID 798269:10 found in tblMainRegArticle but not in localMainRegArticle.

## Root cause (suspected):

The EnsureProductExistsLocally method in ProductPageController (added in DEV-5/PR 12509 for flex application restriction lists) calls CopyProductFromRootToLocal, which copies product data into tbl\* tables only. It does not create a corresponding localMainRegArticle dummy row.

When an integration then:

1. Calls GetAllProducts - finds the product in tblMainRegArticle (returns it)
2. Calls UpdateProductFields with that art_no - finds it in tblMainRegArticle, but fails when trying to load from localMainRegArticle → throws exception

The error appearing intermittently and resolving on retry supports this - other code paths (UI actions, inventory operations) eventually call CreateDummyIfNeeded which creates the missing local row.

title: Chemsoft Customer Tool to reveal actual status of documents
description:
iChemistry contains all the products requested by customer and it also contains data if product requested product is in queue, ready for entry or done etc.

However we need to create transparency cross the systems and reveal which of these products actually are found from clients Chemsoft database.

CustomerTool API should be enhanced with new endpoints to list all requested products, status and its current CSNumbers from clients Chemsoft database.

Key identifiers listed below

Localrequested (DocPropsID) / MultiDB (ArtNo)

MultiDB_Explizit (CustomerID ArtNo)

Chemsoft_ExplizitDB (ArtNo, ChemID, CustomerGUID)

Chemsoft_CustomerDB (CustomerGUID, ChemID)

We could also utilize the DocumentStatus database table to show inventory startdate.

Establish freshdesk-mcp in platform env
description:
Read-only MCP server for Freshdesk customer data. Exposes tickets, contacts, companies, and agents via 10 tools. Deployed on the platform cluster with HTTP transport.

Needs a reasonable authentication solution. Going for Microsoft Entra login as a multi-tenant app. 

Every user needs to have a Freshdesk account and use their personal access token for access. 

## Steps

- [X] Code: `intersolia/freshdesk-mcp` — stdio + HTTP transport (Streamable HTTP, `PORT=3000`)
- [X] Create GitHub repo: merge [github-repos PR #211](<https://github.com/intersolia/github-repos/pull/211>) → Terrateam applies → repo created
- [ ] Platform deployment: merge [argocd-applications PR #490](<https://github.com/intersolia/argocd-applications/pull/490>) → `platform-freshdesk-mcp` ArgoCD app
- [ ] Create secret in AWS Secrets Manager at `service/freshdesk-mcp` with `FRESHDESK_DOMAIN` 
- [ ] Ensure auth is ok
- [ ] Ensure MCP is easy to configure and only works when a user has a valid Freshdesk account

## Usage (after deploy)

Connect via MCP client at `http://freshdesk-mcp.freshdesk-mcp.svc.cluster.local/mcp`

title: Creation of an app service on mainop.cloud tenant and sso client on Intersolia tenant for new price list generation tool
description:
Hi John and Henrik,

All the work we have done on the new customer tool has been really inspiring and has opened up for some new possibilities that will make life easier for Jörgen and for the sales teams. Specifically, we have planned for a small web app that can generate price lists with the most recent product data from HubSpot and display it to sales.

Benefits:

* 

Jörgen won't have to create price lists for the different teams and save them as pdfs on SharePoint whenever changes are made.
   \*
 Sales will have access to the most recent information about product pricing and can do searching and filtering better in a web app than in a pdf document.

Requirements:

* 

A web app service on the mainop.cloud tenant that I can deploy to from Azure DevOps. It needs a small node backend to fetch information from HubSpots APIs and create the page. The API token will be stored securely in the Azure deployment pipeline and injected when building the app.
   \*
 An SSO client on the Intersolia Sweden AB tenant that can be used for the Microsoft MSAL authentication process such that only Intersolia employees can see the price lists.
   \*
 A suitable custom domain.

Perhaps we can have a quick chat about it tomorrow?

description:
When replacing a product, the product type should carry over to the selected product, but an error seems to have occurred in some replacements.

After investigation, it turns out that the product type has not been correctly transferred from the previous SDS to the new SDS in all cases.

In Chemsoft, there are two different tables that control which product types are linked to a product, but the procedure has only populated one of them, which causes the product types not to be displayed.

Updating and/or adding a new Phrase is an unecessarily time consuming task and there is room for a lot of improvement. 

We need to set up a service that that captures changes in the .xml files and performs automatic updates/inserts to the database without having to manually add phrases to the xml, create .sql inserts/updates and do this manually in dev and production.

title: Add SessionState ReadOnly to page-load controllers to enable parallel requests
description:
All controllers serving the initial page load use the default SessionStateBehavior.Required, which holds an exclusive write lock on the [ASP.NET](<http://ASP.NET>) session. This forces every request to queue behind the previous one, creating

a strict serial waterfall across 4 phases - even though none of these controllers write to session state.

A 4KB response from GetPhrases takes 16.7 seconds not because of processing, but because it's waiting for the session lock held by earlier requests.

title: Several critical bugs and missing features in the SDS Data API
description:
## 1) New feature: Precautionary Statements schema with placeholder substitutions

POST: client sends Code and may send Substitutions (optional). PhraseTemplate not provided / ignored.
GET: API returns Code + server-emitted PhraseTemplate + Substitutions (if any were provided/stored).
PhraseTemplate is determined by server catalog for the Code and is not persisted from client input.

### Schema (GET shape; Code enum as specified)

```
"Precautionary Statements": {
  "type": "array",
  "items": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {
      "Code": {
        "type": "string",
        "enum": [
          "",
          "P101","P102","P103","P201","P202","P210","P211","P212","P220","P221","P222","P223","P230","P231",
          "P231 + P232","P232","P233","P234","P235","P235 + P410","P240","P241","P242","P243","P244","P250",
          "P251","P260","P261","P262","P263","P264","P270","P271","P272","P273","P280","P280.1","P280.2",
          "P280.3","P280.4","P281","P282","P283","P284","P285","P301","P301 + P310","P301 + P312",
          "P301 + P330 + P331","P302","P302 + P334","P302 + P335 + P334","P302 + P350","P302 + P352","P303",
          "P303 + P361 + P353","P304","P304 + P312","P304 + P340","P304 + P341","P305","P305 + P351 + P338",
          "P306","P306 + P360","P307","P307 + P311","P308","P308 + P311","P308 + P313","P309","P309 + P311",
          "P309 + P312","P310","P311","P312","P313","P314","P315","P320","P321","P322","P330","P331","P332",
          "P332 + P313","P333","P333 + P313","P334","P335","P335 + P334","P336","P336 + P315","P337",
          "P337 + P313","P338","P340","P341","P342","P342 + P311","P350","P351","P352","P353","P360","P361",
          "P361 + P364","P362","P362 + P364","P363","P364","P370","P370 + P372 + P380 + P373","P370 + P376",
          "P370 + P378","P370 + P380","P370 + P380 + P375","P370 + P380 + P375 + P378","P371",
          "P371 + P380 + P375","P372","P373","P374","P375","P376","P377","P378","P380","P381","P390","P391",
          "P401","P402","P402 + P404","P403","P403 + P233","P403 + P235","P404","P405","P406","P407","P410",
          "P410 + P403","P410 + P412","P411","P411 + P235","P412","P413","P420","P422","P501","P501.1","P502","P503"
        ]
      },
      "PhraseTemplate": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Server-emitted canonical phrase for this Code, containing placeholders like {0}, {1}, ..."
      },
      "Substitutions": {
        "type": "array",
        "description": "Optional. Values substituted into placeholders in PhraseTemplate.",
        "items": {
          "type": "object",
          "additionalProperties": false,
          "properties": {
            "Index": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 0 },
            "Value": { "type": "string" }
          },
          "required": ["Index", "Value"]
        }
      }
    },
    "required": ["Code", "PhraseTemplate"]
  }
}
```

### example

POST

```
"Precautionary Statements": [
  { "Code": "P210" },
  { "Code": "P260", "Substitutions": [{ "Index": 0, "Value": "dust/fume/gas/mist/vapours/spray" }] }
]
```

### POST contract

Required per item: Code
Optional per item: Substitutions (if omitted, treat as “no variable inserts provided”)
If PhraseTemplate is included in POST, it must be ignored (or rejected); it must not be stored as client-provided data.

### Rules

Index specifies which placeholder to substitute.
Rendering is deterministic from server PhraseTemplate + Substitutions (no stored rendered sentence).

### Acceptance criteria

POST persists Code and persists Substitutions when provided.
GET returns Code + server PhraseTemplate + stored Substitutions (when present).
Client-sent PhraseTemplate is not returned verbatim unless it matches the server canonical template.

---

## 2) Bug: MFactorAcute / MFactorChronic not persisted despite being posted

### Current behavior

Values are populated and posted successfully, but stored values end up null; response contains no error/warning.

### Example payload fragment:

```
"MFactorAcute": 100,
"MFactorChronic": 0
```

### Acceptance criteria

Posting MFactorAcute=100 and MFactorChronic=0 persists.
Subsequent GET returns 100 and 0 (not null).

---

~~Hazard code H280 does not trigger pictogram GHS004.~~

### ~~Acceptance criteria~~

~~If hazard statements include H280, resulting pictograms include GHS004.~~
~~No other pictogram trigger logic changes in this issue.~~

### Required change

```
[
  "",
  "Gas","Liquid","Solid","Other","Paste","Aerosol","Granulat","Viscous liquid","Grains","Pellets","Shavings",

title: Replace Base64 document transfer with real, signed download links
description:
OpenAccess currently transfers documents as Base64-encoded strings from the backend to the client. Once the document has been received and reconstructed on the client, the download is initiated by **programmatically simulating a user click** (e.g. creating a blob URL and triggering a click on an `<a>` element).

This approach relies heavily on browser-specific behavior and is increasingly unreliable.

### Problem

Simulating user interaction to trigger downloads **does not work consistently across browsers and platforms**.

Known issues include:

* Some browsers block programmatic downloads that are not initiated by a real user gesture
* Inconsistent behavior between Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari, and mobile browsers
* Downloads silently failing or being blocked without user feedback
* Increased fragility due to browser security changes and tightened anti-abuse measures

Combined with Base64 transfer, this results in:

* Large in-memory payloads
* Complex client-side reconstruction logic
* Browser-dependent hacks to initiate downloads
* Poor reliability and user experience

### Root Cause

The root issue is that the system is **trying to fake a browser download** after the file has already been transferred as data, instead of letting the browser perform a native HTTP download.

### Proposed Change

Replace Base64 document transfer and simulated click downloads with **real, signed download links**.

The API should:

* Return a **time-limited, signed HTTPS URL** for the document
* Let the browser handle the download natively via normal navigation or fetch
* Eliminate the need for client-side click simulation entirely

The signed download link should:

* Be short-lived and securely signed
* Enforce authorization and access control
* Support standard HTTP download behavior

title: "Same key already has been added" error after previewing application in review
description:
Issue: Duplicate JSON keys cause "same key already has been added" error on save

Summary

After clicking "Visa" / "Show" to preview the application in the review form, all subsequent save operations fail with the error "same key already has been added to the list".

Steps to Reproduce

1. Open an application review
2. Click "Show" to preview the application request
3. Close the preview modal
4. Make any change (e.g., add or remove a document)
5. Click Save

Expected Behavior

Save completes successfully.

Actual Behavior

Server returns 500 error. Exception occurs in [ASP.NET](http://ASP.NET) MVC's JsonValueProviderFactory during model binding before the controller action is reached.

Root Cause

The JSON payload sent to the server contains duplicate keys. Both indexed format (approvalGroups\[0\], approvalGroups\[1\]) and array format (approvalGroups: \[...\]) exist simultaneously in the same request.

This is caused by the form-users-populated event in ApplicationReview_v3.vue:

eventBus.$on('form-users-populated', (event) => {

```
this.data.dynamic.approvalGroups = event;
```

});

When the preview modal loads, it triggers component initialization that emits this event, overwriting approvalGroups with an array. The existing indexed properties (approvalGroups\[0\], etc.) from the original data remain, causing duplicate keys when serialized to JSON.

title: FD293394 - Unable to update application review
description:
# Issue

When saving a review of an application that has existing documents, the system throws "Unable to read document data from session store" error.

# **Root Cause**

A previous fix (PR 12539 / DEV18) added validation to throw an error when document data cannot be read from session. However, the CreateDocument method is called for ALL documents (both newly uploaded and existing), not just new uploads. Existing documents have a Guid set (persisted from their original upload) but their file data is no longer in the session - it was uploaded in a previous session when the document was first created.

title: FD293901: Solve root cause of restriction list data missing for random products for multiple customers
description:
I've confirmed repeated incorrect states of restriction list / substance data issues. Can we monitor how common this is and make changes to CPU to cover these scenarios so that our data self-heals? Use monitoring of the issues to see that changes we make has an impact.

Facts known:

It's common that data related to why a product is on list is missing in customer systems. (references to substances)

I've seen two variations of incorrect states for this, one where the product still have references to old version of substances in the product data, but restriction list data is updated and referring to the new substances.

In the other case data is missing but for correct substances.

There's a known issue for the case where the customer uses product application and picks a product from the global database, let's see that as a separate issue. This task is about the cases where the customer have the product active in inventory.

Scenario 1:

```sql
-- Here's a query to detect one of the cases. Would be good to monitor all customer systems for this issue:
select top 10000 prod.ArtNo, prod.ModifiedDate, mainprod.ModifiedDate, *
from GetInventory(0, 10) inv
join tblMainRegArticle prod
on inv.AttID = prod.ID and inv.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
left join MultiDB..localMainRegArticle mainprod
on prod.ID = mainprod.ID and prod.ID_mdbID = mainprod.ID_mdbID
where exists (
	select *
	from MultiDB..localDocLnkCAS multi
	where multi.AttID = prod.ID and multi.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
	and not exists (
		select *
		from tblDocLnkCAS localsubst
		where localsubst.AttID = multi.AttID and localsubst.AttID_mdbID = multi.AttID_mdbID
		and localsubst.CASAttID = multi.CASAttID and localsubst.CASAttID_mdbID = multi.CASAttID_mdbID
	)
)
and not mainprod.ModifiedDate > prod.ModifiedDate -- try to exclude products that recently have been updated by SDS team and not updated locally yet.
```

Scenario 2:

```
-- Here's a query to detect one of the cases. Would be good to monitor all customer systems for this issue:
select top 10000 prod.ArtNo, prod.ModifiedDate, mainprod.ModifiedDate, *
from GetInventory(0, 10) inv
join tblMainRegArticle prod
on inv.AttID = prod.ID and inv.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
left join MultiDB..localMainRegArticle mainprod
on prod.ID = mainprod.ID and prod.ID_mdbID = mainprod.ID_mdbID
where exists (
	select *
	from MultiDB..localMainLnkLists l
	where l.AttID = prod.ID and l.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
	and not exists (
		select *
		from tblMainLnkLists locall
		where locall.AttID = l.AttID and locall.AttID_mdbID = l.AttID_mdbID
		and locall.ListID = l.ListID and locall.ListID_mdbID = l.ListID_mdbID
	)
)
and not mainprod.ModifiedDate > prod.ModifiedDate -- try to exclude products that recently have been updated by SDS team and not updated locally yet.
```

If needed we can discuss strategies for monitor + making tweaks/fixes and follow impact on the fixes.

PS. It might be worth it to take a look at the timing of SDS Tools and CPU to make sure we don't have an issue if SDS team makes more than one change within a CPU cycle.

description:
See [SLS-50](https://linear.app/intersolia/issue/SLS-50/fd277356-investigate-broken-links-to-uploaded-files)

Today there's a scenario where file gets temporarily stored in session, and somehow lost, if file can't be fetched from session we store the temporary link to the session object instead of a valid URL.

Either we validate and warn the user on form submit, or less nice solution is to at least throw a real error to the user (meaning they'd have to redo the application).

In contrast to what has sometimes happened now, where we allow broken links that are persisted, that the user later expects to work.

title: FD277260: Unable to download documents on certain devices (OpenAccess)
description:
Users are unable to open or download documents (e.g., safety sheets) on some mobile devices, primarily Android. The issue seems related to how the client currently handles file downloads.

At the moment, the client creates a download link programmatically using a base64-encoded document string and then simulates a click to trigger the download. This approach appears to fail or behave inconsistently on Android devices, where the file either doesn’t download at all or produces no visible result.

**What we know:**

* The issue occurs mainly on Android devices.
* iOS seems less affected.
* Several client-side error handling approaches have been tested, but the issue persists.
* The underlying cause is likely how base64 data URLs are handled on mobile browsers when used to simulate downloads.

**Next step / Proposed solution:**
Refactor the download flow so that documents are **served directly from the backend** instead of being returned as a base64-encoded string.

* Implement a proper download endpoint returning a binary stream with correct `Content-Type` and `Content-Disposition` headers.
* The client should receive a real URL to the file and trigger a standard download request.
* This should ensure consistent behavior across all devices and browsers.

title: FD277035: RA Signed tab can load data from wrong organisation node when coming from Overview
description:
If you use Overview and click on any of the Todo-numbers for risk assessment and then click on a sub-department that is different from the organisation node you selected in the tree, then risk assessment list, signed tab will use wrong organisation IDs, resulting in a different data set compared to doing the equivalent operation in risk assessment module.

See image for example:

![image.png](https://uploads.linear.app/d040f0e9-edba-4253-a021-5028751c4dfb/38bea43e-c4d3-47e2-ae6d-bc1cb815ade4/6cf7d646-89e1-449c-b461-1e095fc657c1)

This worked before dev team introduced new UI for riskassessment and/or dataaccess (that is currently turned off for RA Signed Tab.

Additionally this code from RiskAssessment_v3.vue, line 140 is suspected to be involved in the mistake:

```
    var node = (this.fromPage && this.orgId) ? node = { KeyID: this.orgId.split(':')[0], KeyID_mdbID : this.orgId.split(':')[1] } : window.SideTree.GetSelectedNode();
    axios.get(`/RiskAssessmentList/${this.signedRisksURL}?orgID=${node.KeyID}&orgID_mdbID=${node.KeyID_mdbID}`)
```
